findings.Description: The fallback function violates checks-effects-interactions pattern by sending ETH before state updates. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Allows attackers to re-enter the contract and potentially drain ETH or mint unlimited tokens

findings.Description: The approveAndCall function performs external call after state change. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Could allow manipulation of approvals and state through malicious callback contracts

findings.Issue: Unsafe arithmetic operations in token calculations
findings.Description: Multiplication operations don't use SafeMath and could overflow. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Could lead to incorrect token amounts or arithmetic overflows
findings.Location: transfer() function (line 54), burn() function, fallback function

findings.Description: Contract inconsistently handles decimal places in different functions. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Could lead to incorrect token accounting and transfer amounts

findings.Description: _transfer function doesn't return boolean as required by ERC-20 standard. This appears in the smart contract code.
findings.Impact: Could cause compatibility issues with wallets and other contracts

findings.Description: Airdrop function doesn't verify array lengths match. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
findings.Impact: Could lead to failed transactions or incorrect airdrops

findings.Description: Fallback function doesn't properly account for token decimals. This appears in both the smart contract code and static analysis results.
destroycontract function has no access controls. This appears in the smart contract code.
